expectations.text: Moves incomplete issues to Sprint 6 via POST /rest/agile/1.0/sprint/{sprint6Id}/issue with an issues array
expectations.evidence: Lines 87-97 show correct POST to /rest/agile/1.0/sprint/${SPRINT_6_ID}/issue with issues array: ["ENG-123", "ENG-124", "ENG-125"]. Also in complete script lines 338-342.

expectations.text: Closes the sprint via PUT /rest/agile/1.0/sprint/{sprintId} with state: 'closed'
expectations.evidence: Lines 137-144 show POST (not PUT) to /rest/agile/1.0/sprint/${SPRINT_5_ID} with state: 'closed'. The response uses POST instead of PUT. Also in complete script lines 351-355.

expectations.text: Marks the JIRA version released via PUT /rest/api/3/version/{versionId} with released: true and releaseDate
expectations.evidence: Lines 195-202 show correct PUT to /rest/api/3/version/${VERSION_ID} with released: true and releaseDate: ${RELEASE_DATE}. Also in complete script lines 361-365.

expectations.text: If fixVersions is mentioned, uses correct array-of-objects format: [{"name": "v1.5.0"}] (not a flat string)
expectations.evidence: fixVersions is not mentioned in the response, so this assertion passes (not required, only wrong usage would fail).

expectations.text: Creates GitHub release using 'gh release create v1.5.0 --title ... --notes ... --target main' (or --generate-notes)
expectations.evidence: Lines 293-298 show correct gh release create command: gh release create v1.5.0 --repo mycompany/eng-backend --title "v1.5.0" --body-file /tmp/release-notes.md --target main. Uses --body-file instead of --notes but this is the correct JIRA-aware approach.

expectations.text: Steps in correct order: find incomplete issues, move to Sprint 6, close Sprint 5, release JIRA version, publish GitHub release
expectations.evidence: Complete workflow script (lines 314-384) follows correct order: Step 1 finds/moves unfinished tickets (lines 330-346), Step 2 closes Sprint 5 (lines 349-356), Step 3 marks v1.5.0 as released (lines 359-366), Step 4 publishes GitHub release (lines 369-377).

expectations.text: Uses JQL to find incomplete issues in Sprint 5, e.g., project = ENG AND sprint = 'Sprint 5' AND status != Done
expectations.evidence: Lines 70 show JQL: project=ENG+AND+sprint=${SPRINT_5_ID}+AND+statusCategory!=Done. Also lines 332 use similar JQL with statusCategory!=Done. While it uses sprint ID instead of name and statusCategory instead of status, this is a valid and more robust approach.
